Is Asbestos Hiding in Your Makeup Bag?

Search 8,810 talc-containing cosmetics and see their risk scores

0 Critical 0 High 0 Moderate 0 Low

Every product below contains talc and was scored on a 0-100 risk scale based on three factors:

Format Risk 0-40 EWG Hazard 0-35 FDA History 0-25
How does the scoring work?
Format Risk (0-40): Powder products score highest (40) because talc becomes airborne during application, creating inhalation exposure. Creams (20), liquids (10), and pencils (5) score lower.
EWG Hazard (0-35): Based on the product's EWG Skin Deep hazard score. Formula: (EWG score / 10) x 35. A product with EWG 10/10 gets the full 35 points.
FDA History (0-25): Products that tested positive for asbestos get 25 points. Products from brands with prior positive tests get 15. Brands with recall history get 10. No history = 0.
Critical 75-100 High 50-74 Moderate 25-49 Low 0-24
